title: Copy an Important Document
summary: Make a photocopy or digital copy of an important document.
learnerPromise: You will be able to make a clear, complete copy of a document using a copier, scanner, or phone camera.
whyItMatters: Having copies of important documents helps you fill out forms, apply for services, and recover faster if the original is lost or damaged. Copies also let you share documents without risking the original.
realLifeUses: A school or job application asks for a copy of your ID or transcript
You want a backup of your insurance card or medical records
You need to mail a document but want to keep a copy for yourself
You are applying for a program and need to submit copies rather than originals
materialsNeeded: The document you want to copy
Access to a copier, scanner, or smartphone with a camera
Optional: a copy app or email to send the digital file
result: A clear, complete copy of one important document stored digitally or printed.
youWillLearn: How to make a copy using a photocopier or scanner
How to take a clear photo of a document with a phone
How to check that the copy is complete and readable
How to store or share the copy safely
miniChallenge: Pick one important document and make a copy of it. Check that every word and detail on both sides is clear and readable.
steps: Choose a document that would be useful to have a copy of
Place it flat on a copier or scanner, or on a well-lit surface if using a phone
Make the copy and check that all text, numbers, and images are clear
If the document has a back side, copy that too
Store the digital copy in a safe folder or put the printed copy somewhere secure
completionCriteria: The copy includes all pages or both sides of the document
The text and details on the copy are clear enough to read
commonProblems: The copy is blurry or cut off — make sure the document is flat and all edges are on the glass or in the camera frame
The copy is too dark or light — adjust the copier settings or lighting if using a phone
You only copied one side — check if the document has information on the back
tips: Use a copier app on your phone for automatic edge detection and straightening
If copying at a library or store, ask staff for help if you are unsure how the machine works
Keep digital copies in a password-protected folder or secure cloud storage
